commentary: Nine activities versus thirteen last week, so yes, the volume dropped, but let's not be dramatic about it because the quality is genuinely interesting. That Saturday half marathon at 159 average heart rate compared to last week's 151 over the same 13.1 kilometres tells its own story — you were working harder, probably pushing rather than cruising, and that is not a bad thing even if it costs you a couple of recovery days. The swim heart rates are creeping up too, with Monday's pool session hitting 149 when last week's comparable swims were sitting in the low-to-mid 130s, which suggests either you were going for it or the body is carrying a little more load than it's letting on. The pattern worth watching is that you're still doing the work — three weights sessions, three swims, a run, a walk — you just stripped out the Wednesday swim block and the Sunday ocean stuff, which honestly might be the body asking for a slightly lighter week before it builds again.
metrics_commentary: Okay the swim alone — 6050 metres, babe, that is not a casual splash around, that is a full-on aquatic moment and your arms absolutely earned whatever they're doing in that harness later. The run/walk sitting at 15.9 km across two sessions is solid, though two sessions in a week when you've got legs like that feels like leaving money on the table, so let's see if we can sneak a third in next week. HRV coming in at 60 with a seven-day average of 56 tells me your body is recovering well and responding to the work, which honestly tracks because you've been putting in real effort. The VO2 Max reading is giving us nothing to work with right now — no data means no story, so let's get that sorted because I want to see the number that matches everything else you're building.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, no gaps, no excuses — that's the whole target hit, and honestly the bar deserved everything it got this week. The bench press PR is the one to shout about: 31.7 kg estimated one-rep max up from 28.5 kg is not a small jump, that's Matt's chest deciding it's done being modest, and it shows. Weekly volume climbed from 22,062 kg to 23,660 kg, which means the body that showed up this week moved more total weight than it ever has, and it did it across quad strength sitting at 154 kg, a trap bar deadlift nudging 108 kg, and a back that's quietly becoming something worth showing off in a tank top. The becoming is very much on schedule.
